'''Jeigh Esse''' {{Pronounced|Jay ess}} was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Stellan (species)|Stellan]] who was cast off-[[Stellan (planet)|world]] at an early age when it was discovered that she had an aggressive temperament. She later became a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] after having been raised by a group of [[Rodian/Legends|Rodians]]. Esse distinguished herself in her career by [[Death/Legends|killing]] her targets with some of the more unorthodox weaponry at her disposal.

==Biography==
Jeigh Esse was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Stellan (species)|Stellan]], who was born with an aggressive temperament. As this was at odds with the passive nature of the Stellan, she was launched off [[Stellan (planet)|her homeworld]] and into [[Realspace/Legends|space]], as was the custom for her people whenever a child proved to be particularly menacing. She was later discovered by [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] and raised by them. Esse eventually became a bounty hunter as well.<ref name="daa" />

==Personality and traits==
Jeigh Esse maintained a menacing appearance, which matched her demeanor. She was born an overly aggressive being, which was at odds with the more passive nature of her people, although exceptions had been known to arise in the past. She relished dispatching her enemies with the more unique items in her arsenal, and took satisfaction from a job well done. Esse possessed pale white skin.<ref name="daa" />

==Equipment==
Jeigh Esse boasted a wide array of weapons in her arsenal, but the most unique of them all was her [[polarizer]], which could high-energy charges several [[Meter/Legends|meters]] ahead of the wielder. It was adapted from a [[BlasTech Industries/Legends|BlasTech Industries]] [[EG-7 blaster]]. Esse also made use of a standard [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]].<ref name="daa" />

==Behind the scenes==
The character of Jeigh Esse was created by [[Chris Cowdrill]] through the "[[Design An Alien]]" contest held in the [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine|''Star Wars Galaxy'']] magazine. The contest began in [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 3|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 3]] ([[1995]]), and Cowdrill, aged 15, submitted his entry, which was later published in [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 7|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 7]] ([[1996]]), having won second place in the "Under 16 years old" subdivision. The winner of that division was the [[Critokian]] design. [[Leland Chee]], the ''[[Star Wars]]'' continuity editor, later confirmed that the published "Design an Alien" material was to be considered [[canon]].<ref name="tt">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=690#14724917|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-05-03|time=5:45 PM|quote='''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine #7 published the winners of the 'Design an Alien' contest, where fans were encouraged to submit artwork and backstories of new SW aliens. Are these aliens canon? [...]''' Yes, though any text discrepencies with other sources would likely to superceded by the other source.}}</ref>
